description: Letter from Eli b. Yosef, from Alexandria, to Barhun b. Salah al-Tāhartī, Fustat. Around 1055. The writer is from Safukas, and his main trading good is oil that is exported from the Maghreb to Egypt. The writer asks the addressee to take care of an inheritance of his relative, Avraham al-Safakusi, who has two sons – Abu al-Faraj and Ishaq. Ishaq’s guardian is Nehoray. (Information from Gil, Kingdom, Vol. 4, #713) VMR
description_he: מכתב מאלי בן יוסף, אלכסנדריה, לברהון בן צאלח אל-טאהרתי, פוסטאט, כ-1055, על שמן מהמגרב ועל ירושת אברהם אל-ספאקוסי
